name: Orlan-MKS
tagline: The latest of a 50-year line of Russian rear-entry EVA suits
description: Latest variant of the Orlan family — Russian rear-entry EVA suit that has flown continuously since Salyut 6 in 1977. The cosmonaut steps in through a backpack-mounted hatch that locks shut in one motion, eliminating the airlock pre-breathe required by lower-pressure US suits. Operating pressure 5.7 psi pure O₂. Used on the ISS Russian segment and the legacy Mir and Salyut stations.
best_known_for: The latest variant of the Orlan family — Russian rear-entry EVA suit, in service since the 1977 Salyut program
